messages.content:
We saw a young man _______ a meal.
a) to sit down and order
b) sit down and order
c) had sat down and orde
b) sit down and order
It is important for students ________ of themselves emotionally.
a) to take care
When I get up early, I like to listen to the birds _______.
c) singing

What is in this letter? Why don`t you let me _______ it?
a) to have read
b) to read
c) read
b) to read

need the money _______ my phone bills.
a) to have paid
b) pay
c) to pay
c) to pay

It is good for you ________ jogging every morning.
a) going
b) to go
c) go
b) to go

Would you ________ a cup of tea?
a) have liked
b) like
c) to like
b) like

Lily has to work at three jobs _______ herself and her four children.
a) to support
b) for support
c) to have supported
a) to support

Jack is not a child anymore and can _______ after himself.
a) have looked
b) to look
c) look
c) look

My parents always let me _______ late on Saturday and Sunday.
a) sleep
b) to sleep
c) have slept
a) sleep

Tim will be here at six. His boss is going to let him _______ the job early.
a) left
b) leave
c) to leave
b) leave

I would like to suggest a few ideas for you _______ about.
a) thinking
b) to think
c) think
b) to think

Unfortunately, the table was too small for all of my friends _________.
a) to sit round
b) sit around
c) had sat round
a) to sit round

Let`s _______ to the cinema tonight.
a) to go
b) go
c) going
b) go

. I have arranged ________ my mother to visit my new office.
a) by
b) for
c) of
b) for

We could ________ to a night club if you don`t mind.
a) went
b) go
c) to go
b) go

The program about teenagers was so funny. It made me _______ .
a) to laugh
b) laugh
c) had laughed
b) laugh

Megan heard someone _______ the door.
a) had closed
b) to close
c) close
c) close

Kate went to Chicago _______ her childhood friends.
a) to visit
b) visit
c) to have visited
a) to visit

John went out _______ football with his friends.
a) to have played
b) to play
c) playing
b) to play
